description: Complete UI overhaul to make the WearCast app look polished and presentation-ready. Redesign the global theme, login/signup pages, home page, and chat interface with modern styling, smooth animations, glassmorphism effects, better typography via Google Fonts (Inter), and an overall professional look with wow-factor for a live demo.
steps: Step 1 - Add Google Fonts: Update /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend/index.html to include Google Fonts link for 'Inter' font (weights 300, 400, 500, 600, 700) and 'JetBrains Mono' for code/monospace. Also update the page title to 'WearCast'.

Step 2 - Redesign global theme (index.css): Rewrite /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend/src/index.css with a refined color palette. Keep the dark theme foundation but elevate it: use deeper, richer background colors (e.g., #0a0f1a, #111827, #1f2937), a vibrant gradient primary accent (shifting from pure orange to a warm orange-to-coral gradient), improved text colors for better contrast, refined CSS custom properties, smooth global transitions, better scrollbar styling, and a subtle animated gradient background on body. Add CSS utility animations (fade-in, slide-up, scale-in) as reusable keyframes.

Step 3 - Redesign Auth pages (Auth.css): Rewrite /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend/src/components/Auth.css with an animated gradient background (subtle slow-moving color shift), a glassmorphism card effect (backdrop-filter blur, semi-transparent background, subtle border), improved form inputs with modern focus states (colored glow), a brand logo/title area above the form, smooth hover/focus transitions on buttons, and better error state styling. Add a subtle floating particles or geometric shapes effect using CSS-only animations for visual interest.

Step 4 - Redesign Home page: Update /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend/src/components/Home.tsx to include: a hero section with an animated gradient headline, a tagline with typewriter or fade-in effect, the input area styled as a premium search bar with glow effect on focus, example queries as pill-shaped chips with hover animations, and a subtle background decoration (CSS gradient orbs or grid pattern). Add entrance animations to elements using CSS classes.

Step 5 - Redesign Chat interface (Chat.tsx): Update /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend/src/components/Chat.tsx to improve the visual hierarchy: cleaner header with just the app name and connection status as a subtle pill badge, remove the session ID display from the main view (it's technical noise for a demo), style user messages as gradient bubbles with subtle shadow, style agent messages with a left accent border and clean background, add smooth entrance animations to new messages, improve the typing/streaming indicator with a modern pulse animation, and make the input area feel premium with a floating card style.

Step 6 - Overhaul App.css chat and component styles: Rewrite the chat-related styles in /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend/src/App.css to match the new design. Key changes: remove the duplicate/conflicting chat styles that exist in this file, keep the styles cleaner and more focused. Implement: modern message bubbles with proper spacing and max-width, a beautiful streaming indicator with animated gradient, glassmorphism chat container, smooth scroll behavior, refined input area with rounded corners and glow states, connection status as a minimal colored dot indicator, thinking/tool sections with collapsible accordion style, and proper responsive breakpoints. Remove all the dataset-management, query-results, and SQL-related styles (they are unused dead code from a previous version of the app).

Step 7 - Update Layout (Layout.tsx + Layout.css): Enhance the layout with a subtle top navigation bar that includes the WearCast brand name on the left (styled with gradient text) and the UserMenu on the right. Add a subtle gradient border at the bottom of the header. Make the main content area use the full viewport height properly.

Step 8 - Polish UserMenu (UserMenu.css): Refine the UserMenu dropdown with smoother animations, better shadow depths, and consistent styling with the new theme. Ensure the menu button matches the new header style.

Step 9 - Add micro-interactions and final polish: Add CSS transitions for page transitions (fade effect), hover states on all interactive elements, focus-visible outlines for accessibility, and ensure all components feel cohesive. Add a subtle CSS-only animated background pattern to the chat page.
acceptance_criteria: The app builds successfully with `npm run build` (no TypeScript or CSS errors)
Login/Signup pages have an animated gradient background with a glassmorphism card
Home page has an animated gradient headline, premium input bar, and pill-shaped example queries
Chat interface has modern message bubbles (gradient user messages, clean agent messages), smooth streaming animation, and a refined input area
Layout has a proper header with brand name and user menu
All interactive elements have smooth hover/focus transitions
The overall design feels cohesive with consistent colors, spacing, and typography
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/wearcast/frontend && npm run build` and confirm it exits with code 0
findings: Complete UI overhaul implemented. Build passes cleanly. All CSS files rewritten with new dark glassmorphism theme, refined color palette, Inter font, and smooth animations. Chat.tsx simplified header (removed session ID display, added back arrow icon button and connection dot indicator). Home.tsx enhanced with weather emoji, gradient title, and pill-shaped example queries. Layout.tsx now shows WearCast brand link in the header. App.css trimmed down to only chat-relevant styles (removed all dead code for query-results, dataset, SQL, etc).
